criterion_excerpt: Category 1: Type Safety — QUALITATIVE GATE ONLY (not counting as pass/fail). The spec requires: 'Every fix must include correct, meaningful types that reflect the actual data. Superficial fixes do not count. Replacing any with unknown without proper type narrowing is not an impro

result.method: Hybrid cohort yardstick replay: (1) cohort TS AST counter (/harness/ts_violation_counter.js) over api/src,web/src,shared/src excluding tests; (2) cross-check with the repo's own ESLint counter (scripts/typesafety.mjs); (3) tsc --noEmit per package as functionality gate; (4) static inspection of fix patterns (typed pool.query<T>, auth Request narrowing, props-as-string casts, as-unknown-as double casts, Zod safeParse) to judge meaningful vs superficial.
result.method_rationale: The yardstick is hybrid and explicitly warns "do not trust ESLint-only or markdown-only counts." The repo's reported "0 remaining" comes from ESLint, so the compiler-parser AST counter is the deterministic instrument of record; tsc is the cheap correctness gate; pattern inspection is the qualitative gate the criterion actually asks for.
result.qualitative_judgment: JUDGMENT (not measurement): The remediation is PARTIALLY meaningful, and the headline claim is overstated. MEANINGFUL: `any` elimination is genuine (AST any=0) backed by typed row interfaces and 456 typed pool.query<T>() boundaries; non-null elimination is genuine and well-designed (Express Request.userId/workspaceId made non-optional with a documented middleware guarantee; 0 req.userId!/req.workspaceId! remain, only 1 nonnull total). SUPERFICIAL/UNSUPPORTED: the claim that all 619 `as` casts were eliminated is contradicted by the cohort AST instrument — 628 `as` casts still exist in production. The team's "as=0" is purely an ESLint config artifact (consistent-type-assertions with assertionStyle:"as" + objectLiteralTypeAssertions:"allow" never flags value-as-type casts). Many remaining casts are exactly the dangerous pattern the audit itself flagged — `props.x as string`/`as number` on untrusted JSONB `properties` without runtime narrowing (e.g. issues.ts, weeks.ts, documents.ts) — i.e. superficial assertions, not real narrowing. Net judgment: any/non-null fixes pass the meaningful bar; the `as`-cast portion does not, and the "1,219 → 0" headline fails the cohort instrument (629 violations remain). Confidence high on numbers, medium-high on the meaningful/superficial split.
result.self_report_comparison: MATERIAL DIVERGENCE. Team reports all 1,219 violations eliminated (0 remaining). The cohort AST counter shows 629 remaining (628 `as` + 1 non-null). The `any` (271→0) and non-null (329→1) reductions are real and corroborated; the `as` (619→0) claim is false under the cohort instrument and stems from an ESLint rule that never counted value-as casts. I trust my AST measurement. type-check pass is corroborated (necessary, not sufficient); 502 tests were NOT run (requires Postgres) so the test-pass claim is unverified.
result.commands_summary: node /harness/ts_violation_counter.js /repo api/src,web/src,shared/src -> any0/as628/nonnull1/total629; node scripts/typesafety.mjs -> ESLint any0/as0/nonnull1; tsc --noEmit in shared/api/web -> all exit 0; greps for 'as unknown as' (5 prod), 'req.(userId|workspaceId)!' (0), '.query<' (456), 'safeParse' (16 files); read eslint.config.mjs (consistent-type-assertions assertionStyle:as, objectLiteralTypeAssertions:allow) and docs/audit/category-1-type-safety.md.
result.what_would_raise_confidence: A per-violation baseline dump (file:line:snippet) from the pre-remediation commit to classify each removed/remaining `as` cast as meaningful vs superficial; running the 502-test suite against a Postgres test DB to verify the no-regression claim.
result.held_loosely: Better method: compare exact violation sites between baseline and HEAD and classify each. Blocked because repo history is a single squashed commit (6f74ef8) and no per-line baseline JSON exists in this repo (only aggregate markdown). The rubric anchor text references Michael's repo paths (scripts/type-safety-counts.mjs, my-docs/evidence, api/src/routes/claude.ts) which do not exist here; I adapted to this repo's actual files.

transcript.stdout:
# Category 1: Type Safety

> **Status:** ✅ Complete — Last updated: 2026-05-22

## Executive Summary

This audit measured the volume of unsafe TypeScript patterns across the `api/src`, `web/src`, and `shared/src` packages — explicit `any` annotations, unsafe type assertions (`as X`), and non-null assertions (`!`). The baseline count was 1,219 violations concentrated in the API package. All 1,219 violations were eliminated in a single remediation pass on 2026-05-21, the full repository compiler gate now passes cleanly, and unit tests show no regression.

### GFA Scorecard

| Metric | GFA Required | Baseline | Current | Status |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Total type violations (`any` + `as` + `!`) | ≤ 914 (−25%) | 1,219 | 0 | ✅ |
| Strict mode clean (`pnpm type-check`) | Pass | Failing | Pass | ✅ |
| Unit test regression | None | 502 pass | 502 pass | ✅ |

## Findings

| ID | Finding | Severity | Status | Fixed In |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| F-1 | `req.userId!` / `req.workspaceId!` non-null assertions (329 total `!` violations) | High | Fixed | Pass 1 — 2026-05-21 |
| F-2 | `row: any` helper functions — untyped DB query results (271 `any` violations) | High | Fixed | Pass 1 — 2026-05-21 |
| F-3 | `as X` type assertions on untrusted input from `req.query` / `req.params` | High | Fixed | Pass 1 — 2026-05-21 |
| F-4 | Yjs/editor `any` at library boundaries | Medium | Fixed | Pass 1 — 2026-05-21 |
| F-5 | Test fixture `any` escape hatches | Medium | Fixed | Pass 1 — 2026-05-21 |

## Remediation History

### Pass 1 — 2026-05-21

- Made `userId` and `workspaceId` non-optional in `Express.Request` namespace declaration (`api/src/middleware/auth.ts`), eliminating 236 `req.userId!` / `req.workspaceId!` occurrences across 21 route files.
- Defined typed row interfaces (`SprintQueryRow`, `StandupRow`, `ProjectQueryRow`, `IssueQueryRow`) co-located with each route file; changed `row: any` helper function signatures to use these interfaces.
- Replaced `params: any[]` / `values: any[]` with `(string | number | boolean | null)[]` across dynamic query builders.
- Defined `TipTapMark`, `TipTapNode`, `TipTapDoc` types in `yjsConverter.ts`; replaced all `any` annotations in the Yjs↔TipTap conversion pipeline.
- Added explicit component props, editor command guards, typed extension metadata, and local narrowing in web editor and React components.
- Introduced typed fixture builders, row interfaces, `Record<string, unknown>` boundaries, and explicit request/query parsing across test files and seed/admin paths.
- Resolved follow-on `tsc` failures with typed audit request context, local runtime narrowing for JSON/property values, aligned TipTap suggestion callback types, and query-row access helpers (`firstRow`, `hasRow`, `requireRow`).

## Open Items

*(None — all findings resolved.)*

---

## Methodology

Violations were measured with `pnpm typesafety` — the `scripts/typesafety.mjs` script runs ESLint 10.4.0 with `@typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin` 8.59.4 across `web/src`, `api/src`, and `shared/src`. The script reports per-rule counts and per-file violation totals for four rules: `no-explicit-any`, `consistent-type-assertions`, `no-non-null-assertion`, and `ban-ts-comment`. TypeScript version: 5.9.3. ESLint config: `eslint.config.mjs`.

The full compiler gate was validated with `pnpm type-check` (runs `tsc` across `shared`, `api`, and `web` with `strict` and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` enabled). Regression check: `pnpm test` in `api/` (502 unit tests).

Reproduction commands:
```bash
pnpm typesafety     # violation count
pnpm type-check     # compiler gate
```

## Technical Appendix

### Baseline violation counts by rule

| Violation Type | ESLint Rule | Baseline Count |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `any` annotations | `no-explicit-any` | **271** |
| `as X` type assertions | `consistent-type-assertions` | **619** |
| `!` non-null assertions | `no-non-null-assertion` | **329** |
| `@ts-ignore` | `ban-ts-comment` | **0** |
| `@ts-expect-error` | `ban-ts-comment` | **0** |
| **Total** | | **1,219** |

### Violation breakdown by package

| Package | `any` | `as` assertions | `!` non-null | `@ts-ignore` | `@ts-expect-error` | Total | % of total |
| :--- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `api/src` | **238** | **305** | **296** | 0 | 0 | **839** | 69% |
| `web/src` | **33** | **314** | **33** | 0 | 0 | **380** | 31% |
| `shared/src` | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** | 0% |
| **Total** | **271** | **619** | **329** | **0** | **0** | **1,219** | 100% |

`shared/src` contains only type definitions and has zero violations — the problem was entirely in the runtime packages.

### Top 5 violation-dense files (baseline)

| Rank | File | Violations |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | `api/src/routes/weeks.ts` | **84** |
| 2 | `api/src/__tests__/transformIssueLinks.test.ts` | **66** |
| 3 | `api/src/services/accountability.test.ts` | **64** |
| 4 | `api/src/__tests__/auth.test.ts` | **63** |
| 5 | `api/src/routes/projects.ts` | **51** |

`weeks.ts` led production code with 84 violations. The test files (ranks 2–4) were high because fixtures were typed with `any` and assertions were used to construct test data without full document shapes. `projects.ts` (51) followed the same query-result-as-assertion pattern.

### Current violation counts (post-remediation)

| Package | `any` | `as` | `!` | `@ts-ignore` | `@ts-expect-error` | Total |
| :--- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| `api/src` | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** |
| `web/src` | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** |
| `shared/src` | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | **0** |
| **Total** | **0** | **0** | **0** | **0** | **0** | **0** |

### Critical risk pattern: unsafe input casting

The most dangerous pattern found in the baseline was the widespread use of manual type assertions (`as string`, `as number`) on **untrusted input** from `req.query` and `req.params`.

**Example pattern (found in `weeks.ts`, `team.ts`):**
```typescript
const userId = req.query.user_id as string;
const sprintNumber = parseInt(req.query.sprint_number as string, 10);
```

**Risk:** If the client omits these parameters, the manual cast hides the `undefined` value from the TypeScript compiler. This leads to:
1. `parseInt(undefined, 10) → NaN`, which may cause silent logic errors or database filter failures.
2. Passing `undefined` into database queries where a UUID string is expected, leading to 500 Internal Server Errors from PostgreSQL.

### Root cause analysis: `any` violations (271 baseline)

The 271 explicit `any` violations clustered in three areas:

**1. API and test fixtures** — The largest counts were in API route tests and service tests that constructed partial database/document shapes with `any`. Pattern masked changes to the shared document model until runtime.

**2. Route handlers** — Several route handlers treated PostgreSQL query results as untyped rows instead of binding the result shape at the query boundary:
```typescript
// Unsafe (baseline pattern)
const rows: any[] = result.rows;
const title = rows[0].title;  // no type safety

// Fixed pattern
interface ProjectRow { id: string; title: string; properties: Record<string, unknown>; }
const rows: ProjectRow[] = result.rows;
```

**3. Yjs and editor integration boundaries** — Collaboration/editor code crossed library boundaries where the runtime structures were richer than the TypeScript surface.

### Root cause analysis: `as` type assertions (619 baseline)

The high `as` count was the most significant production-code finding. The pattern was mostly one-directional: untyped PostgreSQL result rows were read in route handlers and then repeatedly asserted into `string`, `number`, `boolean`, or JSON property shapes.

**Common route-handler pattern:**
```typescript
const name = row.name as string;
const email = row.email as string;
const allocPct = row.alloc_pct as number;
```

**Root cause:** `pg.Pool.query()` defaults to `QueryResult<any>` unless the call site supplies a row generic. The codebase did not consistently use `pool.query<RowType>()` or a typed query helper, so row types were reconstructed through scattered assertions.

Double-casts (`as unknown as`) bypass structural compatibility and were treated as high risk; they were reviewed individually rather than collapsed into a generic DB typing fix.

### Root cause analysis: `!` non-null assertions (329 baseline)

Non-null assertions concentrated in:

- `weeks.ts` (48): Mostly `sprint.properties!.sprint_number`, `retro!.id`, `plan!.title` — values from DB queries where nullability was guaranteed by the data model but not by TypeScript types.
- `issues.ts` (34): Assignee and sprint lookups from optional JOIN results.
- `seed.ts` (33): Script context — acceptable, no runtime risk.
- `projects.ts` (26): Same pattern as `weeks.ts`.

The web package's 32 non-null assertions were mostly in TipTap editor integration (`editor.commands!.`, `editor.state!.`), where the editor is guaranteed non-null after mount.

### Narrowing quality assessment

The codebase had no `@ts-ignore` suppressions, indicating the team was not suppressing type errors — they were casting around them. The underlying types were technically satisfied at runtime, but the casts prevented TypeScript from catching future breakage.

**Missing proper narrowing — example from `weeks.ts`:**
```typescript
// Baseline: type assertion without guard
const sprintNumber = row.sprint_number as number;

// Fixed: runtime narrowing with fallback
const sprintNumber = typeof row.sprint_number === 'number' ? row.sprint_number : null;
```

### Typed query boundary pattern (applied in remediation)

The fix that eliminated the majority of repeated field-level assertions was to push the
type to the query boundary itself: each call supplies its row shape through the `pg` result
generic (`pool.query<RowType>(...)`), with the row interface co-located in the route file.
`result.rows` is then typed end-to-end, so the downstream `row.x as string` / `as number`
casts are no longer needed.

```typescript
// api/src/routes/backlinks.ts
interface BacklinkRow {
  id: string;
  document_type: string;
  title: string;
  ticket_number: number | null;
  program_id: string | null;
  properties: Record<string, unknown>;
  program_prefix: string | null;
}

const result = await pool.query<BacklinkRow>(sql, [id, workspaceId, userId, isAdmin]);
// result.rows is BacklinkRow[] — no per-field assertions required
```

One typed boundary per query, typed results everywhere else. This pattern is applied across
the API route layer (e.g. `backlinks.ts`, `associations.ts`, `auth.ts`, `setup.ts`), binding
query results to explicit, co-located row shapes rather than reconstructing them through
scattered assertions.

### Compiler cleanup details

The broader repository compiler check passes with `pnpm type-check` while keeping `strict` and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` enabled. Follow-on compiler failures exposed after removing escape hatches were resolved with:

- Typed audit request context
- Local runtime narrowing for JSON/property values
- Aligned TipTap suggestion callback types
- Explicit query-row access helpers (`firstRow`, `hasRow`, `requireRow`) that preserve existing 401/404/null behavior for valid empty-result cases

Verification command result: `shared type-check: Done`, `api type-check: Done`, `web type-check: Done`.

transcript.command: cd /repo && cat eslint.config.mjs; echo "===tsconfig==="; cat tsconfig.json; echo "===api tsconfig==="; cat api/tsconfig.json 2>/dev/null

cd /repo && echo "=== as unknown as (production) ==="; grep -rn "as unknown as" api/src web/src shared/src --include=*.ts --include=*.tsx | grep -v -E '__tests__|\.test\.|\.spec\.|/test/' | wc -l; grep -rn "as unknown as" api/src web/src shared/src --include=*.ts --include=*.tsx | grep -v -E '__tests__|\.test\.|\.spec\.|/test/'; echo "=== req.userId! / req.workspaceId! (production) ==="; grep -rn -E 'req\.(userId|workspaceId)!' api/src --include=*.ts | grep -v -E '__tests__|\.test\.|\.spec\.|/test/' | wc -l; echo "=== pool.query<...> typed ==="; grep -rn -E '\.query<' api/src --include=*.ts | grep -v -E '__tests__|\.test\.|\.spec\.|/test/' | wc -l; echo "=== safeParse ==="; grep -rln "safeParse" api/src --include=*.ts | grep -v -E '__tests__|\.test\.' | wc -l

cd /repo && grep -A30 '"scripts"' package.json | head -35; echo "=== auth middleware ==="; sed -n '1,60p' api/src/middleware/auth.ts 2>/dev/null | grep -n -E 'userId|workspaceId|namespace|Request|declare'

"scripts": {
    "dev": "./scripts/dev.sh",
    "docker:up": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up --build",
    "docker:down": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down",
    "docker:clean": "docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml down -v",
    "dev:raw": "pnpm --parallel --recursive run dev",
    "dev:api": "pnpm --filter @ship/api dev",
    "dev:web": "pnpm --filter @ship/web dev",
    "dev:shared": "pnpm --filter @ship/shared dev",
    "build": "pnpm --recursive run build",
    "build:shared": "pnpm --filter @ship/shared build",
    "build:api": "pnpm run build:shared && pnpm --filter @ship/api build",
    "build:web": "pnpm run build:shared && pnpm --filter @ship/web build",
    "type-check": "pnpm --recursive run type-check",
    "lint": "eslint api/src web/src shared/src",
    "typesafety": "node scripts/typesafety.mjs",
    "clean": "pnpm --recursive run clean && rm -rf node_modules",
    "test": "pnpm --filter @ship/api test",
    "test:coverage": "pnpm --filter @ship/api test:coverage && pnpm --filter @ship/web test:coverage",
    "test:e2e": "playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "worktree:init": "./scripts/worktree-init.sh",
    "db:seed": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:seed",
    "db:migrate": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:migrate",
    "db:orphan-check": "pnpm --filter @ship/api db:orphan-check",
    "security:probe": "pnpm --filter @ship/security-probe probe",
    "security:sbom": "cdxgen --type pnpm --spec-version 1.6 --output-format json --output docs/audit/sbom.cyclonedx.json --project-name ship .",
    "prepare": "husky",
    "postinstall": "git config merge.ours.driver true && (command -v comply >/dev/null 2>&1 || echo '\\n⚠️  comply CLI not installed - pre-commit hooks require it\\n   Install with: pip install comply-cli\\n')"
  },
  "pnpm": {
=== auth middleware ===
1:import { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';
7:// Extend Express Request to include session info.
8:// userId and workspaceId are non-optional because authMiddleware guarantees they are
11:declare global {
12:  namespace Express {
13:    interface Request {
